pilot_overview.purpose: Validate experimental design before scaling to full experiments. Pilots use reduced scale: 1 seed, 1024 features, shorter training.
pilot_overview.go_criteria: All pilots must show: (1) convergence, (2) absorption rates span >2x range across variants, (3) downstream metrics show monotonic trend with absorption
pilot_overview.no_go_criteria: If all variants have similar absorption rates (<1.5x span) or downstream metrics show no trend, halt and revise design

pilots.name: Baseline L1 SAE Pilot
pilots.pass_criteria: Converges (loss plateau); L0 within 40-60; absorption rate computable

pilots.name: TopK SAE Pilot
pilots.pass_criteria: Converges; L0 approximately k; absorption rate > Baseline (if H1c holds)

pilots.name: Matryoshka SAE Pilot
pilots.pass_criteria: Converges; both levels active; absorption rate < Baseline (if H1a holds)

pilots.name: OrtSAE Pilot
pilots.pass_criteria: Converges; orthogonality penalty non-zero; absorption rate similar to Baseline (if H1b holds)

pilots.name: Random SAE Control Pilot
pilots.pass_criteria: Absorption rate computable; serves as upper bound

pilots.name: L0-Matched Baseline Sweep Pilot
pilots.pass_criteria: At least 3 L0-matched baselines with L0 within +/- 10% of target

pilots.name: Dose-Response Sparsity Sweep Pilot
pilots.pass_criteria: Absorption spans >2x range; downstream metrics show monotonic trend

pilots.name: Semantic Feature Absorption Pilot
pilots.scale: Use Baseline pilot SAE; 3 semantic categories
pilots.pass_criteria: All categories have computable absorption; rates differ across categories

pilots.name: GPT-2 SAEBench Validation Pilot
pilots.scale: Full SAEBench eval on pretrained GPT-2 small SAE
pilots.pass_criteria: SAEBench completes; absorption score in published range (0.3-0.7)

decision_matrix.partial_go: Proceed with experiments where pilots passed; revise and re-pilot failed directions
decision_matrix.all_no_go: Revise experimental design. Possible revisions: (1) increase feature count, (2) adjust sparsity targets, (3) change synthetic data distribution, (4) add more variants
